items.correct: Shareholders' personal assets are protected if the company fails — they can only lose the amount they invested, not their personal wealth.
items.misconception: Limited liability means the company has limited debts it can take on.
items.random_wrong: Limited liability means a business can only operate for a limited time.
items.why: Limited liability is about the LIMIT on what shareholders can lose, not on what the company can do. Students misread the term as restricting the business rather than protecting investors.

items.correct: An arrangement where a franchisor grants a franchisee the right to use their brand, products and systems in exchange for fees and a share of revenue.
items.misconception: A franchise is when a business owns multiple branches itself.
items.random_wrong: A franchise is when employees buy shares in their company.
items.why: Owned chains are not franchises. A franchise involves separate ownership — McDonald's restaurants are mostly franchised (owned by individual operators), not owned by McDonald's Corporation.

items.q: What is a brand in marketing?
items.correct: A combination of name, design, symbols and associations that distinguishes a product and creates customer perception and loyalty — more than just a logo.
items.misconception: A brand is just the logo on a product.
items.random_wrong: A brand is the company that makes a product.
items.why: Branding is the sum of perceptions, not just visual identity. Coca-Cola the brand is much more than the script logo — it is associations, taste expectations, advertising history.

items.q: Which company type can sell shares on the stock exchange?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Public PLC. “Sole trader” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's Many small online contributions. “A single wealthy investor” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: A PLC issues 200,000 shares. A shareholder owns 30,000. Total dividend pool is £80,000. How much do they receive, in £?
items.why: The real figure is 12000. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
